title: Inverted Pyramid
wikitext:
[]: Inverted Pyramid             Quote Post by Singularity84 » Sun Sep 29, 2013 10:23 pm I was re-watching the original series of NGE, when something caught my eye in episode 22. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Sep 29, 2013 10:52 pm I'm sure the inverted pyramid has some sort of religious significance, the spikes are set up in a pattern, and some of the users on here referred to it as "The Altar" in the NERV/Geofront thread. However, I'm completely lost on the idea myself.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Sep 29, 2013 11:39 pm It's a direct callback to Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water. That inverted pyramid is a copy of the Tower of Babel, both in Tartessos and on Red Noah. It's worth watching Nadia for where he's going here, because you'll find plenty similar and not just the music.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Mon Sep 30, 2013 5:53 am It looks like it's part of a certain imagery regarding heavens/a place where a divinity dwells...

[]:             Quote Post by Cookie H Wilson » Sun Oct 06, 2013 1:37 am Somebody has surely pointed this out already, but nevertheless... I think Anno got inspired by Indian Stepwells: [url]http://subterraneandesign.blogspot.it/2009/11/step-well.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 06, 2013 5:47 am Yep, I think that it had been noted the similarity in the "Geofront/Moon Stuff" topic or the "Things you noticed" one, still good to show this discovery here though. Now I suppose we'll have to wait for the 3.0 CRC to have some info about the inspiration for that inverted pyramid... and pretty much everything else!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 06, 2013 10:57 am Somehow that hadn't come off to me as a stepwell, because the ones I've seen are not always inverted pyramids. There's quite a bit of variation in shape. (Impressive find, though.) The Nadia reference came to mind when I was watching the Nautilus Story part of Nadia, the part when she's telling Jean where she really comes from. Compare the structure they're standing on and the Tower of Babel on Red Noah with the structure as seen from the side (or those lampposts).

[]:             Quote Post by FluffyBunny » Sun Oct 06, 2013 11:47 am UrsusArctos wrote: Somehow that hadn't come off to me as a stepwell, because the ones I've seen are not always inverted pyramids. There's quite a bit of variation in shape. (Impressive find, though.)

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Mon Oct 07, 2013 10:48 am Wasn't there a prison in Azerbaijan or something that was like this?
